title: Jacquiniella teretifolia
intro: Jacquiniella teretifolia, also known as the "Queen of the Andes," is a type of orchid found in the tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Jacquiniella teretifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Jacquiniella teretifolia is commonly used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Jacquiniella teretifolia is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Angraecinae. This species is a type of epiphytic orchid, which grows on other plants for support rather than deriving nutrients from them. Jacquiniella teretifolia is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Jacquiniella is a small group of orchids that are closely related to the genus Aerangis.
